title: Studio UX — faster, more human
prompt: Round 1 · DIVERGE — eight storyboard concepts for making the studio simpler and faster for humans, two per territory: judging boards, moving through phases, taking outputs away, and starting sessions. Each tells a 3-beat story of a person actually using it. Judge the STORY: which of these would make you reach for the tool more often? Select the ones with energy, note what specifically clicks, remix pairs that belong together. Wildcards (sketch-a-seed, voice chips) are deliberately out there — kill them loudly if they miss.

options.label: Flick to judge
options.description: Options come one at a time, phone-card sized — flick right keeps, left kills; the whole pool is triaged in seconds from the couch.

options.label: Duel bracket
options.description: Judging becomes pairwise duels — click the better of two, winners advance through a bracket, and a full ranking emerges from seven clicks with no dials.

options.label: Filmstrip funnel
options.description: A persistent thumbnail strip of every round sits above the board — click any round to jump back, and the strip itself narrows visually toward the crowned winner.

options.label: Phase autopilot
options.description: The studio watches your judging and proposes the next phase as one big glowing button — pressing Enter advances the funnel; you never think about phase names.

options.label: Contact-sheet poster
options.description: Crowning a final auto-composes a shareable poster — the winner large, its lineage tree beneath, the decisive notes alongside — so one artifact tells the whole journey in chat.

options.label: Living shelf
options.description: Every kept option pins itself to a live shelf at the studio's edge; drag a card straight out into your editor or design tool — no export dialog exists.

options.label: Sketch-a-seed
options.description: Start a brainstorm by scribbling a blob instead of typing a brief — round 1 riffs on your sketch's shapes; zero keystrokes from doodle to board. (wildcard)

options.label: Voice + chips kickoff
options.description: Hold space and ramble for fifteen seconds; clarifying questions come back as tappable chips, not text fields — the session is seeded before your coffee cools. (wildcard)

survey.elaborationPrompt: Which of these stories feels like the tool you'd actually reach for daily — and what's missing from all eight?
